a.xlink:title: Repository: gia Branch: feature/clipboard-mcp Commit: 7e7fe8c Fix clipboard output to use plain text unless markdown flag set Date: 8 days ago

Add LLM tool-calling support with clipboard tool registry
- Introduce tools.rs with GiaTool trait and ClipboardTool implementation - Advertise available tools to Gemini and handle tool-use loop in gemini.rs - Enable LLM to request clipboard content (text or image) via function calling
Fix clipboard output to use plain text unless markdown flag set

feat: update dependencies to latest versions 🚀
This commit updates various dependencies to their latest versions to incorporate bug fixes, performance improvements, and new features.
Key updates include:
• anyhow to 1.0.102 • clap and related crates to newer versions • futures and its related crates to 0.3.32 • getrandom to 0.4.2 • image to 0.25.9 • indexmap to 2.13.0 • log to 0.4.29 • nix to 0.30.1 • nom to 8.0.0 • notify-rust to 4.12.0 • num_enum to 0.7.5 • objc2 and related crates to 0.6.4 • once_cell_polyfill to 1.70.2 • openssl to 0.10.75 • pin-project and pin-project-lite to newer versions • rand and rand_core to newer versions • rav1e to 0.8.1 • regex to 1.12.3 • reqwest to 0.12.28 • rustix to 1.1.4 • rustls to 0.23.37 • schemars to 1.2.1 • serde and serde_json to newer versions • smithay-client-toolkit to 0.20.0 • syn to 2.0.117 • thiserror to 2.0.18 • time to 0.3.45 • tokio to 1.50.0 • tracing to 0.1.44 • unicode-width to 0.2.2 • uuid to 1.22.0 • wasm-bindgen and related crates to newer versions • wayland-* crates to newer versions • windows-* crates to newer versions • winit to 0.30.13 • zbus to 5.14.0 • zvariant to 5.10.0
These updates ensure the project utilizes the latest stable and secure versions of its dependencies.
80b186a: feat: update dependencies to latest versions 🚀 (Jochen schieberlein, 8 days ago)

✨ feat: Remove unused notify_rust import
The notify_rust crate was imported but not used in the main.rs file. This commit removes the unused import to clean up the code.

feat(giagui): ✨ Enhance UI and interactivity with Emojis and Hover Texts
• Refactored checkbox labels to use emojis and consolidated descriptions into hover texts for a cleaner UI. • Made the GIA logo clickable to send the current prompt, providing an alternative to the send button. • Improved the appearance and functionality of buttons by adding emojis, hover texts, and specific styling for the send button. • Updated TTS language selector to display selected language with an emoji and more descriptive text. • Added hover texts to all action buttons for better user guidance.
fixup! ✨ feat: Adjust spinner-only window size
abd0682: fixup! ✨ feat: Adjust spinner-only window size (Jochen schieberlein, 4 months ago) ec3bef6: ✨ feat: Adjust spinner-only window size (Jochen schieberlein, 4 months ago)

Brew support (#17)
* Add tar.gz packaging for macOS Intel and ARM builds
This update introduces the creation and upload of tar.gz archives for macOS Intel and ARM binaries in GitHub Actions workflows. It ensures proper versioning for archives and uploads them as release assets. Additionally, it triggers a Homebrew tap update for streamlined distribution.
* Update macOS runner to version 14 in workflow
Bumped the macOS version for the Intel runner from 13 to 14 in the manual-release workflow. This ensures compatibility with the latest macOS environment and keeps the build process up to date.
* Update macOS build workflows to include autotools and pkg-config
Added installation of autotools dependencies (autoconf, automake, libtool) for building macOS binaries. Updated build steps to set PKG_CONFIG_PATH for Opus library detection. Ensures compatibility and successful builds for both Intel and ARM macOS targets.
* -
* Add library and Rust flags for macOS builds
Ensure proper linking of the Opus library by setting LIBRARY_PATH and RUSTFLAGS for both Intel and ARM macOS builds. This resolves potential build issues due to missing library paths.
* Update macOS runner version for Intel build
Changed the runner from macos-14 to macos-15 in the manual release workflow to ensure compatibility and leverage the latest environment. This improves consistency and prepares the workflow for future updates.
* Fix macOS runner specification in workflow

feat: 🍎 Add macOS error notifications and improve error handling
Adds macOS specific error notifications using osascript and falls back to notify_rust for other platforms.
Includes improvements to error handling by extracting the root cause and a more specific error message when a request fails. The clipboard is also cleared if an error occurs and the output mode is set to clipboard.

feat: 🎙️ Add --audio-dialog-text to customize recording prompt
• Add urlencoding dependency to Cargo.lock and gia/Cargo.toml. • Introduce audio_dialog_text field to Config struct. • Implement --audio-dialog-text argument in gia/src/cli.rs for custom dialog messages. • Decode URL-encoded text for the audio recording dialog in gia/src/audio.rs. • Pass audio_dialog_text from Config to record_audio_native function. • Update README.md to document the new --audio-dialog-text option with an example.

feat: ➕ Add --markdown/-M flag to output raw markdown to stdout
Adds a new output option that bypasses the markdown-to-text conversion, allowing raw AI markdown to be piped to external renderers or tools.
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
e99dda4: feat: ➕ Add --markdown/-M flag to output raw markdown to stdout (Jochen Schieberlein, 9 days ago)

feat(gemini): update to gemini-2.5-flash model name
The Gemini 2.0 Flash model has been updated to "Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite". This change affects how the model is referenced in the CLI and GUI. The older "gemini-2.0-flash" and "gemini-2.0-flash-lite" models have been removed from the GUI options. Additionally, the conversation summary now truncates based on character count rather than byte count to ensure accurate representation of multi-byte characters.

602bced: feat: 🎙️ Add --audio-dialog-text to customize recording prompt (Jochen Schieberlein, 9 days ago) 68532a9: feat: 🔇 Don't show error for user-cancelled audio recording (Jochen Schieberlein, 1 month ago)

✨ feat: Consolidate file input handling and documentation
The FILES input source has been unified to simplify the handling of various file types. Instead of separate entries for text and media files, a single FILES entry now encompasses all supported formats, including text, images, audio, video, and PDFs.
The documentation has been updated to reflect this change, clarifying that the -f or --file argument can accept any file extension and that GIA will auto-detect the content type for processing. This change aims to improve user experience by streamlining the input process for file-based operations.

Feature: installation packages for macOS Silicon and macOS Intel (#14)
* Add macOS package builder and installation scripts
Introduced a new directory for macOS package creation, including a `.gitignore`, README, build script (`build-package.sh`), and postinstall script. The installer builds a macOS `.pkg` for the GIA CLI tool, installs binaries to `~/bin`, configures the shell PATH, and removes quarantine attributes.
* Add macOS ARM package build and upload to release workflow
Introduce steps to build and upload a macOS ARM installer package in the GitHub Actions workflow. The `build-package.sh` script now dynamically accepts a version parameter, ensuring version consistency across builds. The release process also includes the macOS ARM package as part of the assets.
* Add macOS package preparation and upload to release workflow
This update introduces steps to build, package, and upload macOS binaries as installers for both Intel and ARM architectures in the manual-release workflow. It ensures the packages are versioned consistently and properly included in the release artifacts. Additionally, the release upload step is updated to handle separate installer files for each architecture.
a3879d8: Feature: installation packages for macOS Silicon and macOS Intel (#14) (mschnecke, 4 months ago)

feat(ci): ⬆️ Install Opus library for release workflows
Adds a step to install the Opus library using Homebrew in both the manual-release.yml workflow. This ensures the necessary dependency is available for builds.

🎨 refactor(icons): Update gia icon assets
- Removed the .ico version of the gia icon. - Updated the .png version of the gia icon. - Added the .xcf (GIMP) source file for the gia icon.
5e31773: 🎨 refactor(icons): Update gia icon assets (Jochen Schieberlein, 4 months ago)

feat(tools): 🔀 Add hybrid search with Gemini grounding support
Implement intelligent search mode selection with user control: - Default: Gemini grounding with Google Search (paid, best quality) - GIA_SEARCH_MODE=duckduckgo: Free DuckDuckGo search - GIA_SEARCH_MODE=brave: Free Brave Search (requires API key)
Key changes: - Replace GIA_SEARCH_API with GIA_SEARCH_MODE for clearer intent - Add google_search_retrieval tool for Gemini models when unset - Automatic fallback to DuckDuckGo for Ollama models - Add logging to inform users about paid vs free options - Update 4 existing tests, add 2 new tests (8 total for search) - Remove unused methods (security_context, is_empty, GiaTool export) - Fix all compiler warnings, format code
Behavior matrix: - Gemini + no GIA_SEARCH_MODE: Google Search grounding (~$0.0005/search) - Gemini + GIA_SEARCH_MODE set: Custom search (free) - Ollama + any setting: DuckDuckGo (free, grounding not supported)
All 108 tests passing, no warnings.
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(notifications): 📢 Add audio recording startup notification
Introduce a system notification to inform users when audio recording begins. This notification is displayed when record_audio is enabled and the spinner is not active, providing a clear indication that GIA is ready to capture audio input.

feat(conversation): Store and utilize API key index for caching
This commit introduces significant improvements to how API keys are managed and utilized, particularly for caching purposes.
Key changes include:
• ConversationMetadata now stores api_key_index: This allows each conversation to remember which API key was used to initiate it. • resolve_conversation selects a random API key for new conversations: When a new conversation is started, a random API key from the available list is chosen, promoting load balancing and preventing overuse of a single key. This index is then stored in the ConversationMetadata. • GeminiClient uses preferred_api_key_index: The GeminiClient is now initialized with a preferred_api_key_index from ProviderConfig. This index is used to ensure that the same API key is used for a given conversation across multiple requests, which is crucial for effective caching. If the preferred index is out of range, it defaults to index 0. • ProviderConfig now includes preferred_api_key_index: This field is passed down to the AI provider implementations. • AiProvider trait includes current_api_key_index(): This new method allows providers to expose their current API key index, facilitating caching strategies. • Enhanced GeminiClient tests: Added tests to verify the handling of preferred_api_key_index, including out-of-range values and ensuring consistent key selection for caching.
These changes collectively improve the robustness and efficiency of API key management, especially for providers that benefit from consistent key usage for caching.
2ba8f5b: feat(conversation): Store and utilize API key index for caching (Jochen Schieberlein, 4 months ago)

✨ feat: Replace FFmpeg with native Rust audio recording (cpal + ogg-opus) (#15)
* ✨feat: Add native audio recording using cpal and hound
This commit introduces a new native audio recording functionality using the cpal and hound crates. This approach avoids the need for external dependencies like FFmpeg for the core recording process, making the application more self-contained and potentially faster.
The new record_audio_native function handles:
• Detecting the default audio input device. • Configuring and building an audio input stream with cpal. • Writing raw audio data directly to a temporary WAV file using hound. • Prompting the user to stop recording via a native dialog. • Finalizing the WAV file. • Converting the WAV file to Opus format using FFmpeg as a post-processing step. • Cleaning up the temporary WAV file.
A fallback mechanism has been added to the record_audio function, which now attempts native recording first. If native recording fails, it gracefully falls back to the existing FFmpeg-based recording method.
The Cargo.toml files have been updated to include the hound dependency.
* fixup! ✨feat: Add native audio recording using cpal and hound
* fixup! ✨feat: Add native audio recording using cpal and hound
---------
Co-authored-by: Jochen Schieberlein <jochen.schieberlein@thinprint.com>

✨ feat: Audio device selection with CLI and environment variable support (#16)
* feat: Add audio device selection with CLI and env var support
- Add --list-audio-devices to display all available input devices - Add --audio-device <DEVICE> to specify recording device via CLI - Add GIA_AUDIO_DEVICE environment variable for default device - Implement priority system: CLI param > env var > default device - Update audio recording functions to accept device parameter - Add helpful error messages when device not found
* docs: Update README with audio device selection features
* feat(audio): Handle WAV writer completion gracefully 🎙️
The WAV writer is now wrapped in an Option and taken at the end of the recording process. This prevents issues if the writer is accessed after the stream has been dropped and ensures that Arc::try_unwrap is not necessary, which could fail if other threads still held a reference.
* feat: 🚀 Add audio resampling and Opus encoding
Introduces audio resampling capabilities using the rubato crate to ensure compatibility with Opus encoding. The audio is resampled to a target rate of 48000 Hz, and the Opus encoder now supports a wider range of input sample rates and channel counts.
This commit also updates dependencies in Cargo.lock and gia/Cargo.toml to include rubato and its related crates for the new resampling functionality.
* feat(audio): Improve audio device listing and selection
This commit refactors the audio device listing and selection logic for better clarity and efficiency.
Changes:
• Optimized Default Device Handling: The default audio input device name is now fetched once before iterating through all devices. This avoids redundant calls to host.default_input_device(). • Consolidated Default Device Printing: The default device is printed only once, improving the output format. • Simplified Device Skipping: The logic for skipping the default device when iterating through all devices has been made more concise and readable. • Introduced find_device_by_name Helper: A new helper function find_device_by_name is introduced to encapsulate the logic of searching for a device by its name. This promotes code reuse and readability. • Enhanced get_audio_device Function: The get_audio_device function now utilizes the find_device_by_name helper, making the device selection process cleaner. • Improved Error Messages: Error messages related to not finding specified audio devices are more informative.
---------
Co-authored-by: Jochen Schieberlein <jochen.schieberlein@thinprint.com>
5439b18: ✨ feat: Audio device selection with CLI and environment variable support (#16) (panjamo, 4 months ago)
